authority.precedence_rule: Repository authority wins over generated reports and historical architecture assumptions.
authority.scope_rule: This contract standardizes state continuity only and does not authorize runtime mutation.

state_vocabulary.unknown_state_rule: Unknown, conflicting, or unmapped states must project to unknown or blocked, never to assigned or running.

packet_contract.description: Packet owns the original work request identity and minimum state needed for downstream projection.

state_source.description: Source file or subsystem used to project current_state.

queue_projection_contract.description: Queue projection owns the normalized scheduling view. It is a read model, not packet mutation authority.
queue_projection_contract.scheduler_eligibility_rule: Only queued records with clear dependencies, safe paths, and non-protected risk may become scheduler_ready_records.
queue_projection_contract.manual_review_rule: Unknown ownership, protected risk, missing state, or forbidden path overlap must route to manual_review_records or blocked_records.

scheduler_input_contract.description: Scheduler owns recommendation and plan output only.
input_rules: risk_level must not equal protected or unknown for autonomous preview routing.
dependencies must be clear before scheduler preview.
forbidden path overlap blocks scheduler eligibility.

worker_resolution_contract.description: Worker resolver owns assignment recommendation only until a future assignment persistence lane.
worker_resolution_contract.unknown_ownership_rule: Unknown ownership must produce manual_review or blocked, not fallback-to-default worker assignment.

validator_evidence_contract.description: Validator evidence owns test and evidence attachment for packet-scoped validation.
hook_rules: V1 reserves validator hooks and does not execute validators.
Protected or unknown risk must block scheduler eligibility until validator and approval hooks are explicit.
Validator evidence must be packet-scoped before approval or commit package consumption.

approval_contract.description: Approval owns human decision state and never self-approves.
approval_contract.human_authority_rule: Approval hooks prepare the human decision only. They do not authorize APPLY, commit, push, merge, deploy, trading execution, or secret access.

commit_package_contract.description: Commit package owns prepared commit evidence only.
rules: Commit package output is recommendation evidence only.
Candidate files must remain inside approved lane boundaries.
Protected, forbidden, telemetry backlog, secrets, and trading paths must be excluded unless a separate human-approved lane explicitly allows them.
Human owner retains commit, push, merge, protected action, and deployment authority.

allowed.required_evidence: valid packet id, safe paths, and no protected blocker
missing required fields, protected path, unresolved blocker, or unsafe goal
scheduler preview and worker resolver recommendation
dependency, risk, ownership, path, or policy blocker
approved execution lane and persisted assignment
missing worker, stale worker, capability mismatch, or unsafe assignment
worker result ready for packet-scoped validation
execution failure, timeout, or unrecoverable worker result
failed validator result requiring human review or retry decision
approval id, packet id, validator evidence, and candidate file list
failure classified for retry, quarantine, or escalation
unmapped state or conflicting source evidence

forbidden: validated -> completed without approval check when approval is required
approval_pending -> approved without human decision evidence

ownership.packet: packet owns original work request identity
ownership.queue_projection: queue projection owns normalized scheduling view
ownership.worker_resolver: worker resolver owns assignment recommendation only until future assignment persistence lane
ownership.commit_package: commit package owns prepared commit evidence only
ownership.human_owner: human owner owns approval, commit, push, merge, protected actions

forbidden_authority: schema does not authorize runtime mutation
schema does not authorize worker dispatch
schema does not authorize queue mutation
schema does not authorize trading execution
schema does not authorize secret access
notes: V1 is a read-model state contract.
Packet files, queue files, scheduler code, worker inboxes, validator outputs, approval inboxes, commit packages, runtime state, and telemetry remain unchanged by this schema.
Later APPLY lanes may build projection and consumer integrations against this contract only after separate human approval.
The next recommended APPLY lane is AIOS-CANONICAL-QUEUE-PROJECTION-APPLY-001.
